title: Rhopaloblaste elmeri
taxonomy: Rhopaloblaste elmeri is a species of tree in the family Arecaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is considered to be an endangered species. Within the family Arecaceae, Rhopaloblaste elmeri belongs to the subfamily Calamoideae. The genus Rhopaloblaste is further classified into the tribe Calameae. Rhopaloblaste elmeri is a monotypic genus, meaning it has only one recognized species.
uses: Rhopaloblaste elmeri is a plant species native to the Philippines, and it has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's bark and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
